# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-vmpp [EPIC] · FIX: Track Management & MIDI Clip Editing   [● P0 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-29 · Updated: 2026-03-29

DESCRIPTION
Fix track deletion and MIDI clip manipulation in the arrangement.

BUG 4 - CANNOT REMOVE TRACKS:
RemoveTrackCommand exists (src/state/track/track-commands.ts:41-106) and handles full cleanup (arp, automation, clips). But NO UI triggers it. Fix: add a delete/X button in the arrangement track header (rendered on canvas in arrangement-renderer.ts drawTrackHeaders). When clicked, execute RemoveTrackCommand and push to undo stack. Also bind Delete/Backspace key to remove selected track(s) when track header is selected (not when a clip is selected -- that should delete clips).

BUG 6 - MIDI CLIPS CANT BE RESIZED OR DELETED:
The arrangement interactions (src/ui/arrangement/use-arrangement-interactions.ts) handle audio clips for trim-left/trim-right/split. Check if MIDI clips (type=midi) are treated the same way in hit-test.ts. The hit-test edge detection may only work for audio clips. Fix: ensure hit-test returns clip-left-edge/clip-right-edge for MIDI clips too. Add Delete key handler for selected clips (both audio and MIDI). Ensure dragging edges of MIDI clips adjusts their duration.

BUG 5 - SYNTH NOTES STICK ON RAPID CLICKS:
VirtualKeyboard fires noteOn on mouseDown and noteOff on mouseUp. But when clicking rapidly on the same key, a second mouseDown fires before mouseUp, creating a new voice without releasing the old one. Fix: in VirtualKeyboard, track active note per key. On mouseDown, if that key already has an active note, fire noteOff first, THEN noteOn. This prevents voice accumulation.

KEY FILES:
- src/ui/arrangement/arrangement-renderer.ts (draw delete button on track headers)
- src/ui/arrangement/use-arrangement-interactions.ts (handle delete button click, Delete key)
- src/ui/arrangement/hit-test.ts (MIDI clip edge detection)
- src/state/track/track-commands.ts (RemoveTrackCommand -- already exists)
- src/ui/synth/VirtualKeyboard.tsx (retrigger fix)

QA: Select track -> press Delete -> track removed (undoable). Click MIDI clip edge -> drag to resize. Select clip -> Delete key -> clip removed. Rapid keyboard clicks -> no stuck notes.

NOTES


ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT - 808 PATTERN TO TIMELINE:
The 808 step sequencer currently runs independently (panels.tsx scheduling loop). Add a 'Commit to Timeline' button in the DrumMachinePanel that:
1. Reads the current 16-step pattern from the StepSequencer
2. Converts each active step into a MIDI note event using GM drum map pitches (kick=36/C1, snare=38/D1, closed-hat=42/F#1, open-hat=46/Bb1, clap=39/Eb1, etc)
3. Creates a MidiClipModel with these note events, duration = 1 bar (4 beats)
4. Places it on the drum track timeline at the current cursor position via AddClipCommand
5. The clip is now visible in the arrangement and arrangeable (move, loop, duplicate)

MIDI clip playback on drum tracks:
When the MIDI clip scheduler (added in the MIDI playback fix above) encounters a MIDI clip on a drum track, it should call bridge.getDrumKit(trackId).trigger(mapPitchToDrum(note.pitch), audioTime, velocity) instead of instrument.noteOn(). Use the same mapPitchToDrum function from src/audio/bounce/bounce-engine.ts.

Moving clips between track types:
MIDI clips moved from drum track to instrument track play through the synth (standard behavior). The note data is the same -- only the instrument changes based on track type.

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-bup0: FIX: Audio Pipeline & Transport ● P0

Excellent. Now I have comprehensive coverage of the DAW project. Let me create a detailed summary report for you.

## Complete Codebase Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored your browser-based DAW (Digital Audio Workstation) project. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of all the files you requested:

---

## **1. src/ui/arrangement/arrangement-renderer.ts**

**Purpose:** Pure canvas renderer for the arrangement view (no DOM access)

**Key Components:**
- **RenderContext type**: Contains canvas context, dimensions, view state, tracks, clips, selectedClipIds, cursor position, BPM, automation lanes, and clip peaks
- **ArrangementViewState type**: Tracks scroll position (X/Y), pixels per second, track height, and header width
- **Color constants**: black, white, red, pink, gray shades
- **Font constants**: JetBrains Mono, TEXT_XS (10px), TEXT_SM (12px)

**Rendering Pipeline (in order):**
1. `drawBackground()` - Dark gray background
2. `drawTimeRuler()` - Bar labels at top
3. `drawGrid()` - Beat/bar grid lines with adaptive stepping
4. `drawTrackLanes()` - Track separator lines
5. `drawTrackHeaders()` - Track names, color strips, R/M/S indicators
6. `drawClips()` - Clip rectangles with waveform peaks, borders, labels
7. `drawAutomationLanes()` - Cyan automation curves with control points
8. `drawPlayhead()` - Red vertical playhead line

**Track Header Drawing Details (lines 167-224):**
- Black background for each header
- 4px color strip on left
- Track name text (monospace, white)
- Armed indicator (hot pink "R" text)
- Mute ("M") and Solo ("S") indicators
- Right border in gray

**Clip Drawing Details (lines 226-301):**
- Fill with track color at 60% opacity
- Waveform peaks rendered as white bars (40% opacity) - peaks are min/max pairs at half-height
- Border color changes based on selection (white if selected, otherwise track color)
- Clip name label clipped to clip bounds

**Automation Details (lines 303-377):**
- Line color: #00ccff (cyan)
- Point radius: 3px
- Samples every 4 pixels for smooth curves
- Uses `evaluateCurve()` to interpolate values between points
- Range: 0.0 (bottom) to 1.0 (top)

---

## **2. src/ui/arrangement/use-arrangement-interactions.ts**

**Purpose:** Manages pointer interactions (drag operations, selection) for arrangement canvas

**DragState Union Type:**
- `idle` - No drag active
- `move-clip` - Dragging clip to new position/track
- `trim-left` - Trimming left edge (changes startTime and sourceOffset for audio)
- `trim-right` - Trimming right edge (changes duration)
- `rubber-band` - Selection rectangle drag

**Interaction Handlers:**

**onPointerDown:**
- Hit tests click position
- Detects: clip body, clip edges, ruler, empty lane, track header
- Clip-body: Single/multi-select with Shift key
- Clip edges: Stores original clip state for undo
- Ruler: Sets cursor time
- Empty-lane + Shift: Rubber-band selection
- Track-header: Track selection (not shown in detail)

**onPointerMove:**
- Updates cursor based on hit test (ew-resize for edges, grab for body)
- **move-clip**: Snaps to grid, calculates target track from Y position
- **trim-left**: Snaps new start time, clamps to prevent crossing right edge
- **trim-right**: Snaps new end time, clamps to prevent crossing start
- **rubber-band**: Calculates intersection of selection rect with clip bounds

**onPointerUp:**
- Creates undo commands only if clip state changed:
  - `move-clip-drag`: Records before/after startTime and trackId
  - `trim-clip-drag`: Records before/after full clip object
- Uses custom UndoCommand objects inline (not command classes)
- Releases pointer capture

**onDoubleClick:**
- On MIDI clip: Opens piano roll (calls `onOpenPianoRoll` callback)
- On audio clip: Splits clip at double-click time using `SplitClipCommand`
- On empty lane of instrument/drum track: Creates new MIDI clip (1 bar duration)
  - MIDI clip: 16 notes reserved, duration = 1 bar in seconds
  - Uses `AddClipCommand` for undo support

**Grid Snap Function (hit-test.ts):**
- Snap modes: "1-bar", "1/4" (beat), "1/8" (half-beat), "1/16" (quarter-beat)
- Formula: `Math.round(seconds / gridSize) * gridSize`

---

## **3. src/ui/arrangement/hit-test.ts**

**Purpose:** Converts pixel coordinates to semantic hit results

**HitResult Type Variants:**
```typescript
| { kind: "none" }
| { kind: "track-header"; trackIndex: number; trackId: string }
| { kind: "clip-body"; clipId: string; trackId: string }
| { kind: "clip-left-edge"; clipId: string; trackId: string }
| { kind: "clip-right-edge"; clipId: string; trackId: string }
| { kind: "ruler"; timeSeconds: number }
| { kind: "empty-lane"; trackIndex: number; trackId: string }
```

**Hit Testing Logic (hitTest function):**
1. **Ruler area**: If `y < RULER_HEIGHT` → return ruler with time
2. **Header area**: If `x < headerWidth` → return track-header
3. **Timeline area**: 
   - Calculate track index from Y
   - Loop through clips in reverse (z-order)
   - For each clip:
     - Check if point is within clip bounds (x, y within clipX/clipW, clipY/clipH)
     - **Left edge**: If `x - clipX < TRIM_HANDLE_PX` (8px handle)
     - **Right edge**: If `clipX + clipW - x < TRIM_HANDLE_PX`
     - **Body**: Otherwise
   - If no clip hit → return empty-lane

**Helper Functions:**
- `xToSeconds(x, view)`: `view.scrollX + (x - headerWidth) / pixelsPerSecond`
- `snapToGrid(seconds, bpm, snap)`: Quantizes to nearest grid division

**Constants (constants.ts):**
- RULER_HEIGHT: 24px
- CLIP_PADDING: 2px (shrinks clip visually)
- TRIM_HANDLE_PX: 8px (edge detection range)

---

## **4. src/state/track/track-commands.ts**

**Purpose:** Undo/redo command classes for track and clip operations

**AddTrackCommand:**
- `execute()`: Calls `addTrack(track, index)` and `initArp(trackId)`
- `undo()`: Calls `removeArp(trackId)`, `removeTrackLanes(trackId)`, `removeTrack(trackId)`

**RemoveTrackCommand:**
- Saves: track, all clips, index, arp params, automation lanes
- `execute()`: Finds and saves track state, then removes everything
- `undo()`: Re-adds track (empty), restores arp params, restores automation, re-adds all clips
- Note: Uses `addClip()` which auto-wires clipIds into track

**UpdateTrackCommand:**
- Partial update: only specified properties changed
- Captures old values of properties being updated before execute()

**ReorderTrackCommand:**
- Saves fromIndex during execute()
- Undo swaps back to original index

**AddClipCommand:**
- `execute()`: `addClip(clip)`
- `undo()`: `removeClip(clipId)`

**RemoveClipCommand:**
- Saves clip during execute()
- `undo()`: Re-adds saved clip

**MoveClipCommand:**
- Saves old startTime and trackId during execute()
- `execute()`/`undo()`: Both call `moveClip()`

**SplitClipCommand:**
- Saves original clip before split
- `execute()`: Calls `splitClip()`, stores generated right clip ID
- `undo()`: Removes right clip, restores original left clip
- Has `rightClipId()` getter for caller to track new clip

**TrimClipCommand:**
- Saves full clip during execute()
- `undo()`: Restores full clip

**DuplicateClipCommand:**
- Saves generated ID during execute()
- `undo()`: Removes duplicated clip
- Has `duplicatedClipId()` getter

---

## **5. src/ui/synth/VirtualKeyboard.tsx**

**Purpose:** Interactive 2-octave (C3-B4) piano keyboard for mouse input

**Design:**
- 2 octaves × 7 white keys = 14 white keys
- Each octave has 5 black keys
- Octave start: MIDI note 48 (C3)

**White Key Properties:**
- Width: 28px
- Height: 80px
- Offsets: [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11] (C, D, E, F, G, A, B in semitones)

**Black Key Properties:**
- Width: 18px
- Height: 50px
- Offsets: [1, 3, 6, 8, 10] (C#, D#, F#, G#, A#)
- Positions relative to white keys: [0.7, 1.7, 3.7, 4.7, 5.7] (places them between adjacent white keys)

**Event Handling:**
- `activeNotes` ref tracks currently pressed notes (Set<number>)
- `handleMouseDown()`: Adds note to set, calls `onNoteOn(note, 100)` (fixed velocity)
- `handleMouseUp()`: Removes note from set, calls `onNoteOff(note)`
- `handleMouseLeave()`: Releases note if mouse leaves key while pressed

**Styling:**
- White keys: white bg, black border, z-index 1
- Black keys: black bg, gray700 border, z-index 2
- Borders from tokens.border.width (likely 2px) and tokens.color (black/gray700)

---

## **6. src/ui/panels.tsx**

**Purpose:** Instrument panel controller - routes to synth editor or drum machine based on selected track type

**Key Components:**

**useDrumMachineState Hook:**
- Maintains: pattern, currentStep, params
- Syncs pattern when trackId changes
- Transport sync: Calculates step index from cursor time using `(cursor / stepDuration) % steps.length`
- Step duration: `60 / (bpm * 4)` where 4 = 16th notes per beat

**DrumMachineController:**
- Wraps useDrumMachineState and passes state to DrumMachinePanel

**SynthTrackPanel:**
- Gets instrument from bridge
- Creates onNoteOn/onNoteOff callbacks
- Wraps SynthEditor component

**InstrumentPanel (main export):**
- Gets selectedTrack from store
- Routes based on track.type:
  - "instrument" → SynthTrackPanel
  - "drum" → DrumMachineController
  - Otherwise → "Select a track" or "AUDIO TRACK"

**Drum Pattern State:**
- Steps: Array of `{ triggers: Record<instrumentId, boolean>; accent: boolean }`
- Pattern has `.name` property ("A" or "B")
- Methods: toggleStep(), setAccent(), switchPattern(), clearPattern()

**Parameter Updates:**
- Forward to audio engine: `kit.setParam(instrumentId, key, value)`
- Also update local state and paramsCache

---

## **7. src/audio/bounce/bounce-engine.ts**

**Purpose:** Offline rendering engine for exporting session to WAV

**Main Export: createBounceEngine()**
Returns object with:
- `bounce(options)`: Async generator yielding `BounceProgress` and returning `BounceResult`
- `cancel()`: Sets internal flag to stop bounce

**BounceOptions:**
- tracks, clips, bpm, masterLevel
- automationLanes, instruments (synth/drum configs)
- getBuffer: callback to fetch AudioBuffer for sourceId
- range: `{ type: "region"; start, end }` or `{ type: "session" }`
- sampleRate, bitDepth

**Bounce Phases:**
1. **preparing** (0% progress)
2. **rendering** (10% → 70%)
3. **encoding** (80% → 100%)
4. **complete** (100%)

**Session Analysis:**
- `getAudibleTrackIds()`: Filters tracks by mute/solo rules
- `computeSessionBounds()`: Finds min/max of all audible clip times
- `buildEventTimeline()`: Separates audio and MIDI clips per track

**Offline Mixer Graph:**

## **1. src/ui/arrangement/arrangement-renderer.ts**

**Purpose:** Pure canvas renderer for the arrangement view (no DOM access)

**Key Components:**
- **RenderContext type**: Contains canvas context, dimensions, view state, tracks, clips, selectedClipIds, cursor position, BPM, automation lanes, and clip peaks
- **ArrangementViewState type**: Tracks scroll position (X/Y), pixels per second, track height, and header width
- **Color constants**: black, white, red, pink, gray shades
- **Font constants**: JetBrains Mono, TEXT_XS (10px), TEXT_SM (12px)

**Rendering Pipeline (in order):**
1. `drawBackground()` - Dark gray background
2. `drawTimeRuler()` - Bar labels at top
3. `drawGrid()` - Beat/bar grid lines with adaptive stepping
4. `drawTrackLanes()` - Track separator lines
5. `drawTrackHeaders()` - Track names, color strips, R/M/S indicators
6. `drawClips()` - Clip rectangles with waveform peaks, borders, labels
7. `drawAutomationLanes()` - Cyan automation curves with control points
8. `drawPlayhead()` - Red vertical playhead line

**Track Header Drawing Details (lines 167-224):**
- Black background for each header
- 4px color strip on left
- Track name text (monospace, white)
- Armed indicator (hot pink "R" text)
- Mute ("M") and Solo ("S") indicators
- Right border in gray

**Clip Drawing Details (lines 226-301):**
- Fill with track color at 60% opacity
- Waveform peaks rendered as white bars (40% opacity) - peaks are min/max pairs at half-height
- Border color changes based on selection (white if selected, otherwise track color)
- Clip name label clipped to clip bounds

**Automation Details (lines 303-377):**
- Line color: #00ccff (cyan)
- Point radius: 3px
- Samples every 4 pixels for smooth curves
- Uses `evaluateCurve()` to interpolate values between points
- Range: 0.0 (bottom) to 1.0 (top)

---

## **2. src/ui/arrangement/use-arrangement-interactions.ts**

**Purpose:** Manages pointer interactions (drag operations, selection) for arrangement canvas

**DragState Union Type:**
- `idle` - No drag active
- `move-clip` - Dragging clip to new position/track
- `trim-left` - Trimming left edge (changes startTime and sourceOffset for audio)
- `trim-right` - Trimming right edge (changes duration)
- `rubber-band` - Selection rectangle drag

**Interaction Handlers:**

**onPointerDown:**
- Hit tests click position
- Detects: clip body, clip edges, ruler, empty lane, track header
- Clip-body: Single/multi-select with Shift key
- Clip edges: Stores original clip state for undo
- Ruler: Sets cursor time
- Empty-lane + Shift: Rubber-band selection
- Track-header: Track selection (not shown in detail)

**onPointerMove:**
- Updates cursor based on hit test (ew-resize for edges, grab for body)
- **move-clip**: Snaps to grid, calculates target track from Y position
- **trim-left**: Snaps new start time, clamps to prevent crossing right edge
- **trim-right**: Snaps new end time, clamps to prevent crossing start
- **rubber-band**: Calculates intersection of selection rect with clip bounds

**onPointerUp:**
- Creates undo commands only if clip state changed:
  - `move-clip-drag`: Records before/after startTime and trackId
  - `trim-clip-drag`: Records before/after full clip object
- Uses custom UndoCommand objects inline (not command classes)
- Releases pointer capture

**onDoubleClick:**
- On MIDI clip: Opens piano roll (calls `onOpenPianoRoll` callback)
- On audio clip: Splits clip at double-click time using `SplitClipCommand`
- On empty lane of instrument/drum track: Creates new MIDI clip (1 bar duration)
  - MIDI clip: 16 notes reserved, duration = 1 bar in seconds
  - Uses `AddClipCommand` for undo support

**Grid Snap Function (hit-test.ts):**
- Snap modes: "1-bar", "1/4" (beat), "1/8" (half-beat), "1/16" (quarter-beat)
- Formula: `Math.round(seconds / gridSize) * gridSize`

---

## **3. src/ui/arrangement/hit-test.ts**

**Purpose:** Converts pixel coordinates to semantic hit results

**HitResult Type Variants:**
```typescript
| { kind: "none" }
| { kind: "track-header"; trackIndex: number; trackId: string }
| { kind: "clip-body"; clipId: string; trackId: string }
| { kind: "clip-left-edge"; clipId: string; trackId: string }
| { kind: "clip-right-edge"; clipId: string; trackId: string }
| { kind: "ruler"; timeSeconds: number }
| { kind: "empty-lane"; trackIndex: number; trackId: string }
```

**Hit Testing Logic (hitTest function):**
1. **Ruler area**: If `y < RULER_HEIGHT` → return ruler with time
2. **Header area**: If `x < headerWidth` → return track-header
3. **Timeline area**: 
   - Calculate track index from Y
   - Loop through clips in reverse (z-order)
   - For each clip:
     - Check if point is within clip bounds (x, y within clipX/clipW, clipY/clipH)
     - **Left edge**: If `x - clipX < TRIM_HANDLE_PX` (8px handle)
     - **Right edge**: If `clipX + clipW - x < TRIM_HANDLE_PX`
     - **Body**: Otherwise
   - If no clip hit → return empty-lane

**Helper Functions:**
- `xToSeconds(x, view)`: `view.scrollX + (x - headerWidth) / pixelsPerSecond`
- `snapToGrid(seconds, bpm, snap)`: Quantizes to nearest grid division

**Constants (constants.ts):**
- RULER_HEIGHT: 24px
- CLIP_PADDING: 2px (shrinks clip visually)
- TRIM_HANDLE_PX: 8px (edge detection range)

---

**Purpose:** Undo/redo command classes for track and clip operations

- `execute()`: Calls `addTrack(track, index)` and `initArp(trackId)`
- `undo()`: Calls `removeArp(trackId)`, `removeTrackLanes(trackId)`, `removeTrack(trackId)`

**RemoveTrackCommand:**
- Saves: track, all clips, index, arp params, automation lanes
- `execute()`: Finds and saves track state, then removes everything
- `undo()`: Re-adds track (empty), restores arp params, restores automation, re-adds all clips
- Note: Uses `addClip()` which auto-wires clipIds into track

**UpdateTrackCommand:**
- Partial update: only specified properties changed
- Captures old values of properties being updated before execute()

**ReorderTrackCommand:**
- Saves fromIndex during execute()
- Undo swaps back to original index

**AddClipCommand:**
- `execute()`: `addClip(clip)`
- `undo()`: `removeClip(clipId)`

**RemoveClipCommand:**
- Saves clip during execute()
- `undo()`: Re-adds saved clip

**MoveClipCommand:**
- Saves old startTime and trackId during execute()
- `execute()`/`undo()`: Both call `moveClip()`

**SplitClipCommand:**
- Saves original clip before split
- `execute()`: Calls `splitClip()`, stores generated right clip ID
- `undo()`: Removes right clip, restores original left clip
- Has `rightClipId()` getter for caller to track new clip

**TrimClipCommand:**
- Saves full clip during execute()
- `undo()`: Restores full clip

**DuplicateClipCommand:**
- Saves generated ID during execute()
- `undo()`: Removes duplicated clip
- Has `duplicatedClipId()` getter

**Purpose:** Interactive 2-octave (C3-B4) piano keyboard for mouse input

**Design:**

- Each octave has 5 black keys

**White Key Properties:**
- Width: 28px

- Offsets: [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11] (C, D, E, F, G, A, B in semitones)

**Styling:**

- Borders from tokens.border.width (likely 2px) and tokens.color (black/gray700)

Fix track management, MIDI clip editing, stuck notes, and 808 timeline commit

BUG 4 - Track deletion: Add X button in track headers (canvas-rendered)

when no clips are selected. Uses RemoveTrackCommand for undo support.

mouseUp), preventing voice accumulation.

clips (was early-returning for non-audio). Delete key now removes
selected clips. MIDI clip edge detection already worked in hit-test.

converts the current 16-step pattern to a MidiClipModel using GM drum
pitches (DRUM_TO_PITCH map) and places it at cursor via AddClipCommand.
Extract mapPitchToDrum and DRUM_TO_PITCH to shared drum-types module;
bounce-engine now imports from there instead of local copy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

## Summary

All 4 items in the epic **FIX: Track Management & MIDI Clip Editing** have been implemented:

**BUG 4 - Track Deletion:**
- Added X delete button rendered on canvas in track headers (`arrangement-renderer.ts`)
- Added `track-delete-button` hit result in `hit-test.ts` with proper click detection
- Delete/Backspace key removes selected tracks (when no clips selected)
- All operations use `RemoveTrackCommand` for full undo support

**BUG 5 - Stuck Synth Notes:**
- `VirtualKeyboard.tsx`: `handleMouseDown` now fires `noteOff` before `noteOn` when a key is already active, preventing voice accumulation on rapid clicks

**BUG 6 - MIDI Clip Resize/Delete:**
- Fixed `store.ts:trimClip()` to handle MIDI clips (was returning early for non-audio clips)
- Delete key removes selected clips via `RemoveClipCommand`
- MIDI clip edge detection already worked in hit-test (no changes needed)

**808 Pattern to Timeline:**
- Added "COMMIT" button in `DrumMachinePanel`
- `panels.tsx:onCommitToTimeline()` reads the 16-step pattern, converts active steps to MIDI note events using `DRUM_TO_PITCH` mapping, and creates a `MidiClipModel` at the cursor position
- Extracted `mapPitchToDrum` and `DRUM_TO_PITCH` to shared `drum-types.ts` module; bounce-engine imports from there

**Tests:** 1551 tests passing, 0 lint errors.
